name:
GOOD FRIDAY
THE PASSION OF THE LORD

lectionary.gospelTitle: The Passion of Our Lord Jesus Christ (Jn 18–19)
lectionary.thematicTag: Good Friday — The Passion of the Lord

readings.summary: The servant of the Lord shall prosper.

readings.citation:
Ps 31:2, 6, 12-13, 15-16, 17, 25
Father, into your hands I commend my spirit.
readings.antiphon: Father, into your hands I commend my spirit.

antiphons.text:
One of the soldiers opened his side with a lance,
and immediately Blood and water flowed out.

planningNotes: FRI 7 PM — single combined celebration, no Mass
Adoration of the Holy Cross with Veneration follows Intercessions
No consecration — pre-sanctified hosts from Holy Thursday

massNotes: Chant before Gospel: Through His Sacrifice, We Are Freed (David Lockwood, SATB, BPM 80) — also used as acclamation during Passion reading (Jn 18:1—19:42)
Solemn Intercessions: Hold Us, Jesus (Chris de Silva, SATB, BPM 60, Choral Dir: Merrick)
Offertory Chant: Lamb of God, What Wondrous Love (Fauré/Kane, arr. Allan Robert Petker, BPM 76, Choral Dir: Merrick)
ADORATION OF THE HOLY CROSS — Acclamation: Behold the Wood (Dan Schutte, SATB, Choral Dir: Tica)

VENERATION & HOLY COMMUNION — Ave Verum (Mozart, SATB, Choral Dir: Merrick) → Pietà (Tom Kendzia & Theresa Donohoo, SATB, BPM 58/80, Choral Dir: Tica) → Behold the Cross (Bob Hurd, BPM 84/91, Choral Dir: Tica, only if needed) → At The Cross / Love Ran Red (Chris Tomlin, BPM 73, Choral Dir: Arielle)
ALT VENERATION — Crux Fidelis (Steven C. Warner, BPM ca.72, Choral Dir: Merrick) in place of Ave Verum | Sunday Is Coming (Phil Wickham, Choral Dir: Arielle) in place of Pietà

responsorialPsalm.setting: Scott Soper • SATB — BPM 66 — Choral Dir: Jeffrey
